recalls.Description: Harbor Freight Tools has announced a recall for the Predator 2000-Watt Power Station (SKU #70084, UPC 193175488718). These portable generators, characterized by their red and black design and marked with 'PREDATOR' in large white letters on the front, can power up to 11 devices simultaneously. They feature multiple outlets including four A/C outlets and three USB ports, and are powered by a 1545-Watt lithium-ion phosphate battery that can be charged through A/C, solar, or car methods. The SKU and UPC can be found on labels attached to the product.
recalls.Title: Harbor Freight Tools Issues Recall for Predator 2000-Watt Power Stations Due to Electric Shock Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Harbor Freight Tools at 800-444-3353 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email recall@harborfreight.com or visit https://www.harborfreight.com/customer-service-product-support.html#5 for further details. More information is also available at www.harborfreight.com under the 'Recall & Safety' section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Predator 2000 Watt Power Station (SKU #70084; UPC # 193175488718)
Location of the SKU and UPC on the recalled Power Station

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of the wires being reversed in EPS mode. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Harbor Freight Tools locations nationwide from February 2025 to April 2025 for approximately $950.

Importers.Name: Harbor Freight Tools, of Calabasas, California

Distributors.Name: Harbor Freight Tools, of Calabasas, California

Hazards.Name: Shock hazard due to reversed wires in AC outlet receptacles during Emergency Power Supply mode.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately and return the power stations to any Harbor Freight Tools location for a complimentary replacement. The company will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Harbor Freight recalls Predator 2000-Watt Power Stations due to shock hazards. Stop using immediately and return for a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations, featuring high-capacity lithium-ion batteries, are being recalled due to serious fire and burn hazards. These power banks include multiple USB and AC outputs, a built-in flood light, and an LCD display. The recall affects several color variants of the HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations, all identified by the model number PS-1000. Affected units were sold by QVC, ZAGG, and ACG from October 2021 to March 2022.

The specific models involved in the recall are Stone Gray, Carbon, Desert Sand, Midnight Blue, and Woodland Green, each with its own SKU and UPC, which can be found on the bottom of the product. Consumers are urged to stop using these power stations immediately and to reach out for a replacement.
recalls.Title: Recall of HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations Due to Fire and Burn Risks; One Fatal Incident Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact the HALO 1000 recall hotline at 888-345-0481, available Monday to Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email halo5528@sedgwick.com. More information can be found online at https://halo1000recall.expertinquiry.com/.

Products.Name: HALO 1000 Portable Power Station - Stone Gray

Injuries.Name: A 79-year-old man in Bradenton, Florida died from smoke inhalation from a fire involving the recalled portable power station in his home in June 2022. In addition to the death, the firms have collectively received four reports of fires involving the HALO 1000 Portable Power Station, including two reports of smoke inhalation, one of which included burn injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen DBK Electronics Co. Ltd.,of China

Retailers.Name: Sold by ZAGG, ACG, and QVC, with approximately 5,740 units sold between October 2021 and March 2022, priced between $1,000 and $1,300.

Hazards.Name: Overheating lithium-ion batteries causing fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the power stations and contact for a free replacement

recalls.metaDescription: HALO 1000 Portable Power Stations recalled due to fire hazards. One death reported. Contact for free replacement.

recalls.Description: Goal Zero is recalling Yeti Link Expansion Modules and EC8 power cables due to potential fire and burn hazards. These products, sold both as kits and individually, are designed for use in homes and vehicles. In vehicles, the modules connect to the alternator to charge the battery while running. For home use, they allow additional batteries to be linked to the Yeti Power Station/Generator. The affected modules are marked with model numbers 98200 or 98210, while the cables include various model numbers such as 98203 and 98204.
recalls.Title: Goal Zero Recalls Yeti Link Modules and EC8 Cables Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled Yeti Link modules and cables immediately and contact Goal Zero at 888-794-6250 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, via email at support@goalzero.com, or visit www.goalzero.com and click on 'Notices/Recalls' for further information.

Products.Name: Yeti Link Expansion Module
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 34,460 (In addition, about 815 were sold in Canada) (The EC8 cables were previously recalled in April 2021)

Images.Caption: Recalled Male EC8 to Ring Cable
Recalled Female EC8 to Ring Cable
Recalled MPPTx to Tank Cable with EC8 Connector

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the cables melting inside a vehicle, causing minor property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: REI, Bass Pro, Batteries Plus, Scheels, Sportsman's Warehouse, and online at GoalZero.com, Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and Backcountry.com

Importers.Name: Goal Zero LLC, of Draper, Utah

Hazards.Name: Overheating and melting of connections posing fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Free replacement kit including a new Yeti Link module and necessary cables with updated connectors and instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Goal Zero recalls Yeti Link Modules and EC8 Cables due to fire hazards. Learn how to get your free replacement kit today.

recalls.Description: Klein Tools and Blackfire have issued a recall for their portable rechargeable power stations, which are equipped with lithium-ion batteries. These power stations can provide up to 1500W of AC power and feature a built-in 983Wh lithium-ion battery pack. They are designed with four 120V outlets, six USB ports, and a 12V car port. The models involved in the recall are PAC1000 (Blackfire) and KTB1000 (Klein Tools), identifiable by labels on the rear of the units. The power stations are orange and black, prominently displaying 'Klein Tools' in white on the front.
recalls.Title: Klein Tools and Blackfire Power Stations Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled power stations immediately and return them to the place of purchase. For further assistance, contact Klein Tools at 800-527-3099 from 7:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.kleintools.com/recall/ktb1000 for Klein Tools units and www.blackfire.com/recall/pac1000 for Blackfire units. More information is also available on their websites under 'Safety Recalls.'

Products.Name: Klein Tools Portable Rechargeable Power Station
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,800 (In addition, about 164 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Klein Tools KTB1000 Portable Rechargeable Power Station
Recalled Blackfire PAC1000 Portable Rechargeable Power Station

Injuries.Name: Klein Tools received four reports of overheating. No injuries have been reported. No lithium-ion battery fires have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through industrial distributors and electrical wholesalers nationwide from November 2021 to November 2023, priced around $1,300

Hazards.Name: Overheating inverter boards leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled power stations and return them to the purchase location

recalls.metaDescription: Klein Tools and Blackfire recall power stations due to fire hazards. Stop using and return to the retailer for a refund.
